N
Nu-Map Docs
Developer Reference
← Back to Nu-Map
nu-map/documentation / dev-portal / nupath-update

NUPath Update Work tab · local only

NUPath Update fetches the authoritative attribute list from tableau.northeastern.edu via a Playwright headless browser, diffs it against the current database, and lets you apply changes. The central design choice is a toggle between Manual (you review each course before applying) and Automatic (GitHub Actions applies everything on a cron schedule without review). That trade-off — control vs automation — is what the tool is built around.

Scan endpoint
GET http://localhost:3333/nupath-scan (SSE stream)
Apply endpoint
POST http://localhost:3333/nupath-fix
Mode endpoint
GET/POST http://localhost:3333/nupath-schedule
Primary source
Tableau (Playwright DOM) — falls back to catalog.northeastern.edu
Cron schedule
Jan 1, May 1, Sep 1 — .github/workflows/update-nupath.yml — currently commented out; toggle via POST /nupath-schedule in the portal

Two modes — the trade-off between control and automation

Mode is persisted on the server (POST /nupath-schedule). When you open the Work tab, the portal fetches the current mode (GET /nupath-schedule) and renders accordingly. Switching mode saves immediately.

flowchart LR subgraph Manual["Manual mode"] direction TB M1["You trigger the scan"] M2["SSE stream — live log + progress"] M3["Inspector appears after scan"] M4["You review, deselect courses you don't trust"] M5["POST /nupath-fix with your selection"] M6["change-log: nupath-manual-fix entry"] end subgraph Auto["Automatic mode"] direction TB A1["GitHub Actions cron: Jan 1 / May 1 / Sep 1"] A2["Same Playwright Tableau scrape"] A3["All changes applied — no review step"] A4["Commits directly to main"] A5["Triggers redeploy (~1 min)"] A6["change-log: nupath-auto-update entry\nwith runId linking to GH Actions run"] end

Risk of Automatic mode: if Tableau returns bad data for a semester, every affected course gets its NUPath silently corrupted and deployed. The Changes tab records each auto-run with a discrepancy list (first 5 shown, full count), so you can audit after the fact. If a bad auto-update lands, run a Manual scan, deselect the wrong entries, and apply only the corrections.

Manual scan — what happens under the hood

sequenceDiagram participant You participant Portal as Browser participant Server as localhost:3333 participant Tableau as tableau.northeastern.edu participant Catalog as catalog.northeastern.edu You->>Portal: click "↺ Scan for Changes" Portal->>Server: EventSource GET /nupath-scan note over Portal: progress bar starts simulated animation\n(setInterval +0.3% every 400ms, caps at 88%)\nbecause real per-course % isn't known until done Server->>Tableau: Playwright headless browser — fetch NUPath data alt Tableau reachable Tableau-->>Server: NUPath attribute set for all courses Server-->>Portal: type:log "Source: Tableau (Playwright …)" else Tableau unreachable / timeout Server->>Catalog: fallback HTML scrape Catalog-->>Server: NUPath from catalog pages Server-->>Portal: type:log "Source: catalog" end loop per course Server->>Server: compare old nuPath set vs new nuPath set Server-->>Portal: type:progress {done, total, msg} note over Portal: real % now available — clears simulated animation end Server-->>Portal: type:done {ok, discrepancies[], scanned, source} Portal->>Portal: npRenderInspector(d)\nall courses pre-selected into npSelected Set\nInspector panel shown

Simulated progress bar: during the Playwright scrape phase, the server has no per-item progress to report — it's doing one big fetch. A setInterval advances the bar to 88% at a fixed rate, then the real type:progress events snap it to accurate percentages once the per-course comparison loop begins. This is cosmetic — it does not affect accuracy.

Three diff types and selective apply

Unlike Catalog Check which uses a type filter, NUPath Update uses individual checkboxes. Every course in the Inspector starts pre-selected (npSelected = new Set(all keys)). You uncheck specific courses before applying.

The three types

TypeConditionMeaning
+ Gainedadded.length > 0 && removed.length === 0Course gained one or more NUPath attributes this semester
− Lostremoved.length > 0 && added.length === 0Course lost one or more NUPath attributes
± Mixedboth added.length > 0 and removed.length > 0Course swapped one attribute for another — inspect carefully

The table columns show old NUPath codes (removed codes in red) and new NUPath codes (added codes in green). Results are sorted by number of NUPath codes affected — most-changed courses first. The dot indicators (●●○) show count at a glance.

Selective apply pipeline

flowchart TD SEL["Inspector — checkboxes per course\nAll pre-selected in npSelected Set"] SEL --> UNCHECK["Uncheck courses you don't trust\nnpToggle(key, false) → removes from npSelected\nSel count badge updates live"] UNCHECK --> APPLY["☢ Apply Selected Fixes\nnpSelected.size must be > 0"] APPLY --> POST["POST /nupath-fix\nbody: [{subject, number, newNuPath}]\nfor each key in npSelected"] POST --> SERVER["Server:\nFor each course: write newNuPath to all-courses.json\nnuPath field only — no other fields touched\nAppend nupath-manual-fix entry to change-log.json\n (fixed count + list of course codes)"] SERVER --> RESULT["Result banner in portal\n{fixed: N, courses: [list]}"] RESULT --> NEXT["npm run build → Preview → push to main"]

Key constraint: NUPath Update writes only the nuPath field. It does not touch title, credits, description, or any other field. If those also need updating, use Catalog Check or a patch file.

Automatic mode — scheduled run details

The cron schedule aligns with Northeastern's semester start dates — NUPath assignments are typically updated before each semester begins.

flowchart TD CRON["GitHub Actions cron fires\nJan 1 / May 1 / Sep 1\n(.github/workflows/update-nupath.yml)"] CRON --> SCRAPE["Checkout repo + npm install\nPlaywright Tableau scrape"] SCRAPE --> DIFF["Compare all-courses.json against Tableau data\nBuild discrepancies list"] DIFF --> APPLYALL["Apply ALL discrepancies\nno human review, no filtering"] APPLYALL --> WRITE["Write all-courses.json\nAppend nupath-auto-update entry:\n {type, runId, triggeredBy:'cron', stats, discrepancies[]}"] WRITE --> COMMIT["git commit + push → triggers deploy.yml\nSite updated in ~1 min"]

The next scheduled date display in the portal is computed by npNextRunDate(): picks the first of Jan/May/Sep strictly after today, falling back to next Jan 1. The currently-upcoming date card is highlighted with an accent color.
